---
title: Implementing the Regular Operations
jupyter: python3
---

So far, we've seen only a trivial regular expression: one containing a single character `칝`, which evaluates to the language {`칝`} $\in 2^{\Sigma^*}$. How do we represent other kinds of regular expressions?

## Concatenation

The operation of concatenation, which we represented using $\circ$, is implicit in putting two characters next to each other. For instance, to represent the regular expression $(\text{칝} \circ (\text{b} \circ (\text{s} \circ (\text{t} \circ (\text{톷} \circ (\text{칝} \circ (\text{k} \circ (\text{툮} \circ (\text{톛} \circ \text{n})))))))))$, we can simply write `칝bst톷칝k툮톛n`.

```{python}
#| code-fold: true
#| code-summary: Load IPA representation of CMU Pronouncing Dictionary

with open("cmudict-ipa") as f:
    entries: list[tuple[str, str]] = [
        l.strip().split(",") for l in f
    ]
    entries: dict[str, list[str]] = {
        w: ipa.split() for w, ipa in entries
    }
```

```{python}
#| colab: {base_uri: 'https://localhost:8080/'}
#| executionInfo: {elapsed: 5, status: ok, timestamp: 1675099877210, user: {displayName: Aaron Steven White, userId: 06256629009318567325}, user_tz: 300}
#| outputId: 5d7bad17-1650-4f75-b254-7ebc79807582
import re

regex_칝bst톷칝k툮톛n = "칝bst톷칝k툮톛n"

string_칝bst톷칝k툮톛n = "".join(entries["abstraction"])

re.fullmatch(regex_칝bst톷칝k툮톛n, string_칝bst톷칝k툮톛n)
```

## Union

In contrast, to represent the regular expression $((\text{칝} \cup \text{톛}) \circ (\text{b} \circ (\text{s} \circ (\text{t} \circ (\text{톷} \circ ((\text{칝} \cup \text{톛}) \circ (\text{k} \circ (\text{툮} \circ (\text{톛} \circ \text{n})))))))))$, which evaluates to {`칝bst톷칝k툮톛n`, `톛bst톷칝k툮톛n`, `칝bst톷톛k툮톛n`, `톛bst톷톛k툮톛n`}, we either use `[]`...

```{python}
#| colab: {base_uri: 'https://localhost:8080/'}
#| executionInfo: {elapsed: 5, status: ok, timestamp: 1675099877210, user: {displayName: Aaron Steven White, userId: 06256629009318567325}, user_tz: 300}
#| outputId: c14e422a-08e0-49f3-d9f8-de4dbc73d9f3
regex_칝톛bst톷칝톛k툮톛n = "[칝톛]bst톷[칝톛]k툮톛n"

string_톛bst톷톛k툮톛n = "".join(entries["obstruction"])
string_칝bst톷톛k툮톛n = "칝bst톷톛k툮톛n"
string_톛bst톷칝k툮톛n = "톛bst톷칝k툮톛n"

(re.fullmatch(regex_칝톛bst톷칝톛k툮톛n, string_칝bst톷칝k툮톛n),
 re.fullmatch(regex_칝톛bst톷칝톛k툮톛n, string_칝bst톷톛k툮톛n),
 re.fullmatch(regex_칝톛bst톷칝톛k툮톛n, string_톛bst톷칝k툮톛n), 
 re.fullmatch(regex_칝톛bst톷칝톛k툮톛n, string_톛bst톷톛k툮톛n))
```

...or an explicit `|`.

```{python}
#| colab: {base_uri: 'https://localhost:8080/'}
#| executionInfo: {elapsed: 170, status: ok, timestamp: 1675101583867, user: {displayName: Aaron Steven White, userId: 06256629009318567325}, user_tz: 300}
#| outputId: 6aa6227b-8dcc-48f2-a330-427a3742ae18
regex_칝톛bst톷칝톛k툮톛n = "(칝|톛)bst톷(칝|톛)k툮톛n"

(re.fullmatch(regex_칝톛bst톷칝톛k툮톛n, string_칝bst톷칝k툮톛n),
 re.fullmatch(regex_칝톛bst톷칝톛k툮톛n, string_칝bst톷톛k툮톛n),
 re.fullmatch(regex_칝톛bst톷칝톛k툮톛n, string_톛bst톷칝k툮톛n), 
 re.fullmatch(regex_칝톛bst톷칝톛k툮톛n, string_톛bst톷톛k툮톛n))
```

Note that the `()` are important in the latter case!

```{python}
#| colab: {base_uri: 'https://localhost:8080/'}
#| executionInfo: {elapsed: 244, status: ok, timestamp: 1675101698294, user: {displayName: Aaron Steven White, userId: 06256629009318567325}, user_tz: 300}
#| outputId: bf78d2b7-fea9-4c49-e587-e281db5346bd
regex_칝톛bst톷칝톛k툮톛n = "칝|톛bst톷칝|톛k툮톛n"

(re.fullmatch(regex_칝톛bst톷칝톛k툮톛n, string_칝bst톷칝k툮톛n),
 re.fullmatch(regex_칝톛bst톷칝톛k툮톛n, string_칝bst톷톛k툮톛n),
 re.fullmatch(regex_칝톛bst톷칝톛k툮톛n, string_톛bst톷칝k툮톛n), 
 re.fullmatch(regex_칝톛bst톷칝톛k툮톛n, string_톛bst톷톛k툮톛n))
```

## Kleene star

Finally, the Kleene star works the way you would expect.

```{python}
#| colab: {base_uri: 'https://localhost:8080/'}
#| executionInfo: {elapsed: 3, status: ok, timestamp: 1675099877211, user: {displayName: Aaron Steven White, userId: 06256629009318567325}, user_tz: 300}
#| outputId: 5b56b60b-b14d-4425-b744-ccafb0acec35
regex_칝칝칝칝bst톷칝k툮톛n = "칝*bst톷칝k툮톛n"

for i in range(10):
    print(re.fullmatch(regex_칝칝칝칝bst톷칝k툮톛n, "칝"*i + string_칝bst톷칝k툮톛n[1:]))
```

To apply the Kleene star to a complex regular expression, we need `()`.

```{python}
#| colab: {base_uri: 'https://localhost:8080/'}
#| executionInfo: {elapsed: 117, status: ok, timestamp: 1675101893835, user: {displayName: Aaron Steven White, userId: 06256629009318567325}, user_tz: 300}
#| outputId: a5fefd60-c072-44d9-cc92-c942953f4ce9
regex_re칝bst톷칝k툮톛n = "(톷i|di)*칝bst톷칝k툮톛n"

for i in range(3):
    print(re.fullmatch(regex_re칝bst톷칝k툮톛n, "톷i"*i + string_칝bst톷칝k툮톛n))
    print(re.fullmatch(regex_re칝bst톷칝k툮톛n, "di"*i + string_칝bst톷칝k툮톛n))
    print(re.fullmatch(regex_re칝bst톷칝k툮톛n, "톷idi"*i + string_칝bst톷칝k툮톛n))
    print(re.fullmatch(regex_re칝bst톷칝k툮톛n, "di톷i"*i + string_칝bst톷칝k툮톛n))
```
